text: Built a new `patch` extension tool to replace the built in one. I'm seeing a lot of code here that does not meet the Lyle ethos. Single line functions, patterns that dont earn their keep. I dont want functionality to change at all, but is it possible to accomplish what we are with fewer lines of code?

I would like to create a similar tool to apply patch from this reference repo. I want it to work nearly the same as far as the outcome of the tool but I would like you to basically pitch the much better, cleaner version of code that we
should implement. I also do not
I would rather do a real repo. This repo has barely anything in it. Pi is considered one of the best repos

Okay so this is going to be a big one for us to really plan thoroughly and come up with a good design and architecture. To be honest, I only have rough idea of what I want to do here, so before we even get into implementation
architecture, I want you to help me figure out and potentially even run tests on what might be best.
